revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Kogo Shūi'''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the Inbe clan. It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] in [[807]].
==Background==
Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed religious services for the court. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthen the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan. He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.
==Contents==
The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor Amenofutodama no Mikoto and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Temmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the text are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. However, there are several events unique to this text so it serves as a supplement to the national histories.
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[Man'yōgana|man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also==
*[[Takahashi Ujibumi]] -- the Takahashi clan records
==References==
* {{cite book | last = Kubota | first = Jun | title = Iwanami Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | date = 2007 | id = ISBN 978-4-00-080310-6 }} * {{cite book | last = Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten Henshū Iinkai | title = Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | date = 1986 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 4-00-080067-1 }} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | date = 1985 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | authorlink = | coauthors = | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | date = 1967 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 4-385-13237-2 }}
[[Category:History books]] <!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. --> [[Category:Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]]
[[ja:古語拾遺]]

{{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the Inbe clan. It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] in [[807]].
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed religious services for the court. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan. He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor Amenofutodama no Mikoto and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Temmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the text are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. However, there are several events unique to this text so it serves as a supplement to the national histories.
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also==
*[[Takahashi Ujibumi]] -- the Takahashi clan records
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]]

[[Category:9th-century history books]] [[Category:Heian period]] [[Category:Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]]
[[fr:Kogo Shūi]] [[ja:古語拾遺]]

{{Multiple issues |refimprove = November 2011 |no footnotes = November 2011 |context = November 2011 }}
{{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the Inbe clan. It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] in [[807]]. {{Shinto}}
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed religious services for the court. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan. He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor Amenofutodama no Mikoto and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Temmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the text are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. However, there are several events unique to this text so it serves as a supplement to the national histories.
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also==
*[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]—the Takahashi clan records
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]]
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | authorlink = | coauthors = | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
{{jmyth navbox long}}
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. -->

{{Multiple issues |context = November 2011 }} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the Inbe clan.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor Amenofutodama no Mikoto and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Temmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the Kogo Shūi are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text, so the Kogo Shūi serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also==
*[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]—the Takahashi clan records
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]]
==Notes==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Kogo shūi | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-11-30 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036}}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 斎部広成 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-11-30 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = Inbe no Hironari | oclc = 683276033}}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 古語拾遺 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-11-30 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = Kogo Shūi | oclc = 683276033}}</ref> }}
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | authorlink = | coauthors = | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}

The historical events described within the Kogo Shūi are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text, so the Kogo Shūi serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />

It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the Inbe clan.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />

However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />

revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{context|date=November 2011}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the Inbe clan.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor Amenofutodama no Mikoto and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |accessdate=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archivedate=2007-08-25 |df= }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |accessdate=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=Japanese |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archivedate=2007-08-25 |df= }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |accessdate=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=Japanese |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archivedate=2007-08-25 |df= }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | authorlink = | coauthors = | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]] *[https://jhti.berkeley.edu/Kogoshui%20copyright.html Searchable text and English translation], [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]]
{{jmyth navbox long}}
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. -->
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kogo Shui}} [[Category:9th-century books]] [[Category:Heian period]] [[Category:Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:807]] [[Category:9th century in Japan]]

{{italic title}} {{context|date=November 2011}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the Inbe clan.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor Amenofutodama no Mikoto and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

{{italic title}} {{context|date=November 2011}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the Inbe clan.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

revision.comment: Moving [[:Category:Imbe clan]] to [[:Category:Inbe clan]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Speedy]]
revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by [[Inbe no Hironari]] (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}

{{jmyth navbox long}}
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. --> {{Authority control}}

This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />

The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.

revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]] *[https://jhti.berkeley.edu/Kogoshui%20copyright.html Searchable text and English translation], [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]]
{{jmyth navbox long}}
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. --> {{Authority control}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kogo Shui}} [[Category:9th-century Japanese books]] [[Category:Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:807]] [[Category:9th century in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto texts]] [[Category:Heian-period books]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kogo Shūi]] {{Inbe Faith}}

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />

The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.

revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]] *[https://jhti.berkeley.edu/Kogoshui%20copyright.html Searchable text and English translation], [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]]
{{jmyth navbox long}}
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. --> {{Authority control}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kogo Shui}} [[Category:9th-century Japanese books]] [[Category:Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:807]] [[Category:9th century in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto texts]] [[Category:Heian-period books]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Kogo Shūi]] {{Inbe Faith}}

However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>

<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}

revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==

<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=draft}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>

<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}

[[en:Kogo Shūi]] [[sn:Kogo Shūi]]
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. -->

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|qq=draft}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Imbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}

revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|qq=}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>

revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[Japan]] written in the early [[Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|qq=}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]] *[https://jhti.berkeley.edu/Kogoshui%20copyright.html Searchable text and English translation], [[Japanese Historical Text Initiative]]

The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.

The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
