revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|qq=}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[Waseda University Library]] *[https://jhti.berkeley.edu/Kogoshui%20copyright.html Searchable text and English translation], [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]]
{{jmyth navbox long}}

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|qq=}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==

<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}

[[en:Kogo Shūi]]
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. -->
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|古語拾遺}} [[de:Kogo Shūi]] [[es:Kogo Shūi]] [[fr:Kogo shūi]] [[id:Kogo Shūi]] [[it:Kogo shūi]] [[ja:古語拾遺]] [[ko:고어습유]] [[uk:Кого Шюї]] [[zh:古語拾遺]]
[[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q531555}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|qq=|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

[[en:Kogo Shūi]]
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. -->
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|古語拾遺}} [[de:Kogo Shūi]] [[es:Kogo Shūi]] [[fr:Kogo shūi]] [[id:Kogo Shūi]] [[it:Kogo shūi]] [[ja:古語拾遺]] [[ko:고어습유]] [[uk:Кого Шюї]] [[zh:古語拾遺]]
{{wikidata link|Q531555}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

[[en:Kogo Shūi]]
<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. -->
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|古語拾遺}} [[de:Kogo Shūi]] [[es:Kogo Shūi]] [[fr:Kogo shūi]] [[id:Kogo Shūi]] [[it:Kogo shūi]] [[ja:古語拾遺]] [[ko:고어습유]] [[uk:Кого Шюї]] [[zh:古語拾遺]]
{{wikidata link|Q531555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, [[:en:Waseda University Library|Waseda University Library]] *[https://jhti.berkeley.edu/Kogoshui%20copyright.html Searchable text and English translation], [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]]

==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}

It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.

The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records

<!-- Although it is an early Heian period text, the large number of man'yōgana vocabulary make it a value Old Japanese resource as well. --> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|古語拾遺}} [[de:Kogo Shūi]] [[es:Kogo Shūi]] [[fr:Kogo shūi]] [[id:Kogo Shūi]] [[it:Kogo shūi]] [[ja:古語拾遺]] [[ko:고어습유]] [[uk:Кого Шюї]] [[zh:古語拾遺]]
{{wikidata link|Q531555}} [[Category:807]] [[Category:9th century in Japan]] [[Category:9th-century Japanese books]] [[Category:Heian-period books]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto texts]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] written in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Japanese imperial court]]. However, at the beginning of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jinmu]] through [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in [[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]] which make it a valuable resource for studying [[:en:Old Japanese|Old Japanese]].
==See also== * [[:en:Japanese Historical Text Initiative|Japanese Historical Text Initiative]] *''[[:en:Takahashi Ujibumi|Takahashi Ujibumi]]''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==

<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q531555]] Wikidata name: Kogo Shūi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} written in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Japanese imperial court}}. However, at the beginning of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei}} in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jinmu}} through {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in {{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|WD=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana}} which make it a valuable resource for studying {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}.
==See also== * {{ill|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|en|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|WD=Q16973361|lt=Japanese Historical Text Initiative}} *''{{ill|Takahashi Ujibumi|en|Takahashi Ujibumi|WD=Q1108567|lt=Takahashi Ujibumi}}''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==

<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}

It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Japanese imperial court}}. However, at the beginning of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei}} in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jinmu}} through {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.

The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in {{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|WD=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana}} which make it a valuable resource for studying {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:古語拾遺]][[de:Kogo Shūi]][[en:Kogo Shūi]][[es:Kogo Shūi]][[fr:Kogo shūi]][[id:Kogo Shūi]][[it:Kogo shūi]][[ja:古語拾遺]][[ko:고어습유]][[th:โคโงะชูอิ]][[uk:Кого Шюї]][[zh:古語拾遺]]
{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} written in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Japanese imperial court}}. However, at the beginning of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei}} in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jinmu}} through {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in {{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|WD=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana}} which make it a valuable resource for studying {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}.
==See also== * {{ill|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|en|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|WD=Q16973361|lt=Japanese Historical Text Initiative}} *''{{ill|Takahashi Ujibumi|en|Takahashi Ujibumi|WD=Q1108567|lt=Takahashi Ujibumi}}''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==
{{reflist|2|refs=
<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} * {{cite book | last = Nishimiya | first = Kazutami | title = Kogo Shūi | publisher = Iwanami Shoten | year = 1985 | language = ja | isbn = 4-00-300351-9 }} * {{cite book | last = Omodaka | first = Hisataka | title = Jidaibetsu Kokugo Daijiten: Jōdaihen | publisher = [[Sanseido|Sanseidō]] | year = 1967 | language = ja | isbn = 4-385-13237-2 }}
==External links== *[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02493/ri05_02493.html Scans] of manuscript, {{ill|Waseda University Library|en|Waseda University Library|WD=Q7971387|lt=Waseda University Library}} *[https://jhti.berkeley.edu/Kogoshui%20copyright.html Searchable text and English translation], {{ill|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|en|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|WD=Q16973361|lt=Japanese Historical Text Initiative}}
{{jmyth navbox long}}

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} written in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Japanese imperial court|1=Emperor of Japan}}. However, at the beginning of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}} in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jinmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} through {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in {{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|WD=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana|1=Man'yōgana}} which make it a valuable resource for studying {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese|1=Old Japanese}}.
==See also== * {{ill|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|en|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|WD=Q16973361|lt=Japanese Historical Text Initiative|1=Japanese Historical Text Initiative}} *''{{ill|Takahashi Ujibumi|en|Takahashi Ujibumi|WD=Q1108567|lt=Takahashi Ujibumi|1=Takahashi Ujibumi}}''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==

<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}

{{italic title}} {{Shinto}} {{nihongo|'''''Kogo Shūi'''''|古語拾遺|}} is a historical record of the [[Inbe clan]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} written in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} (794–1185). It was composed by {{ill|Inbe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe no Hironari|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500712}} (斎部広成) in 807 using material transmitted orally over several generations of the [[Inbe clan|Inbe clan]].<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
==Background== Historically, both the Inbe and [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi]] clans had long performed [[Shinto]] religious services for the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Japanese imperial court|1=Emperor of Japan}}. However, at the beginning of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the [[Fujiwara clan]], whom the Nakatomi clan are a branch of, seized political power. This strengthened the Nakatomi clan while weakening the Inbe clan and caused conflict between the two clans.
Hironari, whose date of birth and death are unknown, wrote this text to clarify the history and legitimize the rights of the Inbe clan as well as to argue the injustice of the Nakatomi clan and decline of the Inbe clan.<ref name="hironari" /> He presented it to {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}} in 807.<ref name="k" />
==Contents== The text consists of three major sections: #The historical events of clan ancestor [[Futodama|Amenofutodama no Mikoto]] and his grandson Amamito no Mikoto. #A description of the [[Japanese creation myth|creation myth]] and the national history from {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jinmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} through {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. #Eleven points of discontent with the Nakatomi clan and the decline of the Inbe clan.
The first two sections serve as evidence and reference for legitimatizing the third and main section.
==Value==
The historical events described within the ''Kogo Shūi'' are nearly identical with those found in ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the oldest written histories of Japan. However, there are several events unique to the text{{specify|date=June 2016}}, so the ''Kogo Shūi'' serves as a valuable source of early Japanese history not found in the national histories.<ref name="e" /><ref name="hironari" />
Linguistically, the text contains a large number of old words written in {{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|WD=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana|1=Man'yōgana}} which make it a valuable resource for studying {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese|1=Old Japanese}}.
==See also== * {{ill|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|en|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|WD=Q16973361|lt=Japanese Historical Text Initiative|1=Japanese Historical Text Initiative}} *''{{ill|Takahashi Ujibumi|en|Takahashi Ujibumi|WD=Q1108567|lt=Takahashi Ujibumi|1=Takahashi Ujibumi}}''—the Takahashi clan records
==References==

<ref name="e">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Kogo shūi |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |oclc=56431036 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="hironari">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=斎部広成 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Inbe no Hironari |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref>
<ref name="k">{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Kokushi Daijiten |title=古語拾遺 |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2012-11-30 |year=2012 |publisher=Shogakukan |location=Tokyo |language=ja |trans-title=Kogo Shūi |oclc=683276033 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=2007-08-25 }}</ref> }}
